title: Phospholipase a2 receptor antigens and their medical use
abstract: The present invention relates to a non-naturally occurring peptide comprising amino acid sequence S-V-L-T-X1-E-N-X2(SEQ ID NO: 1), wherein X1 is any amino acid and X2 is any amino acid, which may optionally be linked to an amino acid sequence X3-I-X4-X5-E-X6 (SEQ ID NO: 5), wherein, X3, X4, X5 and X6 each denote any amino acid. The present invention further relates to the use of such peptides as a medicament such as for the prevention and treatment of kidney disease and in apheresis methods.
claims:
Claims Claims 1. A non-naturally occurring peptide comprising amino acid sequence S-V-L-T-X1-E-N- X2 (SEQ ID NO: 1), wherein X1 is any amino acid and X2 is any amino acid. 2. The peptide according to claim 1 , wherein the peptide is a variant peptide comprising the amino acid sequence SVLTEENC (SEQ ID NO: 2) or SVLTEENS (SEQ ID NO: 3). 3. The peptide of claim 1 or claim 2, wherein the peptide comprises: â¢ A C-terminal domain comprising the amino acid sequence S-V-L-T-X1-E-N-X2 (SEQ ID NO: 1) â¢ An N-terminal domain comprising the amino acid sequence X3-I-X4-X5-E-X6 (SEQ ID NO: 5), wherein, X3, X4, X5 and X6 each denote any amino acid. 4. The peptide according to claim 3, wherein X3 is V or P; X4 is Q, D or E; X5 is S, D or E and X6 is S, D or E. 5. The peptide according to any preceding claim, wherein the peptide is a variant peptide comprising the amino acid sequence S-V-L-T-X1-E-N-X2-K (SEQ ID NO: 6). 6. The peptide of any one of claims 3 to 5, wherein the N-terminal domain comprises the variant peptide comprising the amino acid sequence X3-I-X4-X5-E-X6-L-K (SEQ ID NO: 8). 7. The peptide according to any one of claims 3 to 6, wherein the peptide comprises a linker between the N-terminal and C-terminal domains. 8. The peptide according to claim 7, wherein the linker is selected from a group consisting of: a peptide linker, a synthetic linker, a combination of peptide and synthetic linker. 9. The peptide according to claim 7 or claim 8, wherein the linker separates the N- terminal and C-terminal domains by a distance greater than or equal to a linker consisting of 5 glycine residues. 10. The peptide according to claim 8 or claim 9, wherein the peptide linker comprises 5 glycine residues. 1 1. The peptide according to claim 8 or claim 9, wherein the synthetic linker comprises a PEG molecule. 12. The peptide according to claim 8 or claim 1 1 , wherein the synthetic linker further comprises a lysine residue. 13. The peptide according to any preceding claim, wherein the number of amino acid residues in the peptide is less than or equal to 28 amino acid residues. 14. The peptide according to any preceding claim, wherein the number of amino acid residues in the peptide is less than or equal to 19 amino acid residues. 15. The peptide according to any one of the preceding claims, wherein X1 is L or E and X2 is C or S. 16. The peptide according to any one of claims 3 to 15, wherein the N-terminal domain comprises a variant of the sequence VIQSES (SEQ ID NO: 9) such that one or more of the following substitutions are made: V to P, Q to D or E, optionally either or both S to D or E. 17. The peptide according to any one of claims 3 to 16, wherein the N-terminal domain comprises the sequence PIDDES (SEQ ID NO: 10) or PIESES (SEQ ID NO: 1 1). 18. The peptide according to claims any of one of the preceding claims, wherein the peptide comprises the amino acid sequence X3-I-X4-X5-E-X6-PEG-K-PEG-S-V-L-T-X1-E-N- X2 (SEQ ID NO: 12). 19. The peptide according to claim any of claims 1 to 9 and claims 11 to 18, wherein the peptide comprises PIESES-PEG-K-PEG-SVLTEENC (SEQ ID NO: 13); VIQSES-PEG-K- PEG-SVLTLENC (SEQ ID NO: 14); VIQSES-PEG-K-PEG-SVLTEENC (SEQ ID NO: 15); PIDDES-PEG-K-PEG-SVLTLENC (SEQ ID NO: 16); PI DDES-PEG-K-PEG-SVLTEENC (SEQ ID NO: 17). 20. The peptide according to any preceding claim, wherein the peptide is soluble. 21. A pharmaceutical composition comprising a peptide according to any one of the preceding claims and a pharmaceutically acceptable carrier. 22. The peptide according to any one of the claims 1 to 20 or the pharmaceutical composition according to claim 21 for use as a medicament. 23. The peptide according to any one of the claims 1 to 20 or the pharmaceutical composition according to claim 19 for use in the prevention or treatment of kidney disease. 24. A method of preventing or treating kidney disease in a subject, the method comprising administering a therapeutically effective amount of a peptide in accordance with any one of claims 1 to 20 to a subject in need thereof. 25. The method according to claim 24, wherein the peptide is provided in a pharmaceutical composition according to claim 21. 26. The method according to claim 24 or claim 25, wherein the therapeutically effective amount of the peptide is an amount of the peptide that provides a therapeutically effective inhibition of binding of anti-PLA2R antibodies to native PLA2R in the subject. 27. Use of a peptide in accordance with any one of claims 1 to 20 or a pharmaceutical composition in accordance with claim 21 in the manufacture of a medicament. 28. Use of a peptide in accordance with claim 27, wherein the medicament is for use in the prevention or treatment of kidney disease. 29. A device for extracorporeal treatment of a patient's blood, the device comprising: â¢ a peptide according to any of claims 1 to 20; and â¢ means for separating the peptide from blood. 30. The device according to claim 29, wherein the means for separating the binding partner from blood comprises a substrate of a material selected from the group consisting of: cellulose; cellulose derivatives; agarose; agarose derivatives; polysulphone; polysulphone derivatives; polyacrylamide; polyacrylamide derivatives; and nylon. 31. The device according to claim 29 or claim 30, wherein the device is in a form selected from the group consisting of: hollow fibre cassettes, membranes, and beads. 32. The device according to claim any of claims 29 to 31 , wherein the device is a column. 33. A method of preventing or treating kidney disease in a subject, the method comprising: 3. contacting a volume of the subject's blood with at least 1 peptide according to any one of claims 1 to 20, such that anti-PLA2R antibodies present in the subject's blood are able to bind to and be retained by the peptide; and 4. separating the peptide and bound anti-PLA2R antibody from the blood, to yield an antibody-depleted volume of blood. 34. The method according to claim 33, wherein a batch of blood comprising one or more volumes of blood to be treated, is removed from the subject, and the steps of contacting a volume of the blood with at least 1 peptide, and subsequent separation of the peptide and bound antibodies, completed to yield a batch comprising the volume, or volumes, of antibody-depleted blood. 35. The method according to claim 33, wherein the steps of contacting the blood with the binding partner, and subsequent separation, are carried out "in line". 36. The method according to claim 35, wherein the binding partner is provided in an arrangement so that the patient's blood may flow over the binding partner, thus allowing it to bind anti-PLA2R antibodies in the blood. 37. The method according to claim 33, wherein at least 1 peptide is provided as part of a device according to any one of claims 29 to 32. 38. The peptide for use, method, or device, according to any of claims 22 to 37, wherein the kidney disease is selected from the group consisting of: primary renal failure; membranous nephropathy, such as idiopathic membranous nephropathy or de novo membranous nephropathy; or recurrent membranous nephropathy in a transplant 39. A method of determining levels of anti-PLA2R antibodies in a subject, the method comprising: 

 â¢ contacting at least 1 peptide in accordance with any one of claims 1 to 20 with a sample of a body fluid from the subject, such that anti-PLA2R antibodies present in the subject's body fluid sample are able to bind to and be retained by the peptide; and â¢ determining the amount of anti-PLA2R antibodies retained by the peptide. 40. The method according to claim 39 for use in diagnosing kidney disease in a subject, the method comprising: â¢ determining the amount of anti-PLA2R antibodies in a body fluid sample from a subject in a method according to claim 39; and â¢ comparing the determined amount with a reference value, wherein if the determined amount of anti-PLA2R antibodies in the sample is equal to or greater than the reference value, this indicates that the subject has kidney disease. 41. A method according to claim 40, wherein the subject is one that is considered to be at risk of kidney disease. 42. A method in accordance with any one of claims 39 to 41 that is carried out in vitro. 43. A method according to claim 39 for use in selecting a suitable regimen for the prevention or treatment of kidney disease, the method comprising: â¢ determining the amount of anti-PLA2R antibodies in a body fluid sample from a subject in a method according to claim 39; and â¢ comparing the determined amount with a reference value, wherein if the determined amount of anti-PLA2R antibodies in the sample is equal to or greater than the reference value, this indicates that the subject will benefit from a regimen for prevention or treatment of kidney disease utilising a binding partner for an anti-PLA2R antibody. 44. The method according to claim 43 wherein the subject is an individual diagnosed as having kidney disease, where the cause of the kidney disease has not been determined. 45. The method according to claim 39 or claim 43, further comprising implementing a suitable prevention or treatment regimen that has been selected by the method of the invention. 46. The method according to claim 45, wherein the prevention or treatment regimen utilises at least 1 peptide according to any of claims 1 to 20 for use in the prevention or treatment of kidney disease. 47. The method according to any of claims 43 to 46, further comprising obtaining a value representative of the amount of anti-PLA2R antibodies in the subject's body fluid, and comparing this obtained value with a reference value, wherein if the obtained value is larger than the reference value this indicates that the subject will benefit from a regimen for prevention or treatment of kidney disease utilising a peptide according to any of claims 1 to 20. 48. The method according to claim 39 for use in monitoring effectiveness in a subject of a treatment regimen for prevention or treatment of kidney disease, the method comprising: â¢ assaying a sample of a body fluid of a subject undergoing a treatment regimen for prevention or treatment of kidney disease, to determine the amount of anti-PLA2R antibodies present in the subject's body fluid sample by a method according to claim 39; â¢ comparing the determined amount with a reference value; and â¢ based on this comparison, determining whether the subject's treatment regimen is effective for prevention or treatment of kidney disease. 49. The method according to any claims 40 to 48, wherein the reference value is the mean plus three standard deviations of the amount of anti-PLA2R antibody in approximately 30 samples from normal healthy individuals without kidney disease. 50. The method according to claim 48, wherein the reference value is representative of the amount of anti-PLA2R antibodies present in a comparable sample from the subject at an earlier time point. 51. The method according to any of claims 48 to 50, wherein the subject is a patient undergoing therapy for kidney disease. 52. The method according to any of claims 48 to 51 , wherein, in the event that a proposed treatment regimen is identified as ineffective, the regimen is modified in an attempt to improve effectiveness. 53. The method according to any of claims 48 to 52, wherein, in the event that a proposed treatment regimen is identified as ineffective, the regimen is replaced, and a different regimen tested for efficacy. 54. The method according to any of claims 48 to 50, wherein the subject is a participant in a clinical trial. 55. The method according to any of claims 40 to 54, the method further comprising assessment of one or more additional markers indicative of kidney disease. 56. The method according to any of claims 40 to 55, wherein the body fluid is selected from the group consisting of: blood; serum; plasma; urine; and interstitial fluid. 57. The method according to any of claims 40 to 56, wherein the assay is a quantitative assay. 58. The method according to claim 57, wherein the assay is selected from the group consisting of: enzyme linked immunosorbent assays (ELISAs); western blotting; fluorescent bead-based immunoassays; and immunofluorescence on cell expressed PLA2R. 59. The method according to claim 58, wherein the assay is an ELISA that makes use of an immobilised peptide according to any of claims 1 to 20 to capture anti-PLA2R antibodies in a sample, and a labelled antibody directed to the immunoglobulin class to which the anti- PLA2R antibodies belong to detect the captured anti-PLA2R antibodies.
